mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations. When both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month simultaneously, S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days turn negative.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month in same month
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of co-declining instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 0.5% month-over-month signals expanding supply-side capacity and productivity gains that support corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when INDPRO > 0.5% MoM are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth combined with stable or declining inflation signals a healthy labor market without wage-price spiral pressures. When PAYEMS grows > 0.2% MoM and CPIAUCSL growth remains below 0.3% MoM, S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days are positive.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 3.0% year-over-year triggers expectations of future monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 expanding at an annualized rate above 2.5% signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal combining rising unemployment (UNRATE > 6.5%) with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS < -0.1% MoM) indicates accelerating labor market weakness that triggers equity risk-off behavior and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.
